name: Fennel Gratin with Robiola
recipeIngredient: 2 ounces fresh bread crumbs, lightly toasted under broiler
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 450 degrees.
2. Bring 4 quarts water to boil and add 2 tablespoons salt.
3. Butter 4 small round (4-inch diameter) earthenware gratin or shallow sauce dishes.
4. Halve fennel bulbs and cut into 1/4-inch thick slices. Place in boiling water and blanche until very tender, 8 to 10 minutes. Drain in a colander over sink and set aside until cool enough to handle.
5. Mix tepid fennel with bechamel sauce and fontina cheese and mix well. Divide evenly among 4 gratin dishes and pat down with the back of a spoon.
6. Bake in the top half of the oven for 25 minutes until bubbling and hot. Remove from oven.
7. Place 2 ounces (a dollop or square) of robiola in the center of each dish, sprinkle bread crumbs over robiola, and return to oven for 5 to 6 more minutes until robiola is hot and soft and crumbs have melted.
8. Allow to stand 3 minutes before serving.
Yield: 4 servings
